MINI MINUTES FOR APR-14-25 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 501

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED IN PART WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE PARAGRAPH 24 AND PARAGRAPH 2 OF THE PRAYER FOR RELIEF IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF ALLEGES FACTS SUPPORTING A CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES BY ALLEGING DEFENDANTS KNOWINGLY SUBMITTED A FRAUDULENT BID UNDER PENALTY OF PERJURY IN ORDER TO OBTAIN THE SUBJECT PROPERTY. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE PARAGRAPH 7 OF THE PRAYER FOR RELIEF REQUESTING ATTORNEY'S FEES IS GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE ENTITLEMENT TO ATTORNEY FEES EITHER BY CONTRACT, STATUTE, OR LAW. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER IN ACCORDANCE WITH CRC RULE 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

MINI MINUTES FOR APR-08-25 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 501

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED WITH AND WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. AS A PRELIMINARY MATTER, THE COURT NOTES THAT THIS MATTER WAS CONTINUED FROM APRIL 8, 2025 TO APRIL 14, 2025 FOR PLAINTIFF TO PROVIDE A COURT COURTESY COPY OF THEIR OPPOSITION BY APRIL 8 WITH A COVER LETTER REFLECTING THE NEW HEARING DATE. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO DO SO. CONSEQUENTLY, THE FILED OPPOSITION WAS NOT CONSIDERED. THE DEMURRER TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INTERFERENCE WITH PROSPECTIVE ECONOMIC ADVANTAGE IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE FACTS THAT TEND TO SHOW THE PROBABILITY OF FUTURE ECONOMIC BENEFIT, RATHER THAN A MERE SPECULATIVE EXPECTANCY. (SEE YOUST V. LONGO (1987) 43 CAL.3D 64.) FOR EXAMPLE, IT IS NOT CLEAR WHETHER DRUM WAS THE ONLY BIDDER. THE DEMURRER TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENT INTERFERENCE WITH PROSPECTIVE ECONOMIC ADVANTAGE IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE FACTS SUPPORTING A SPECIAL RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN PLAINTIFF AND EACH INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANT. THE DEMURRER TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATIONS AS TO CIVIL CODE SECTIONS 2924G AND 2024H IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE FACTS SUPPORTING A VIOLATION OF EACH STATUTE WITH REFERENCE TO SPECIFIC SUBDIVISIONS. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND WITH RESPECT TO SECTION 2924M. (SEE CIVIL CODE SECTION 2924M(J).) PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER IN ACCORDANCE WITH CRC RULE 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-14-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Dustin Owens for Happy 42nd Partners, LLC, Plaintiff (via Zoom).
Eric M. Schiffer for Ryan Drum, Defendant (via Zoom).

Mr. Owens contested the court's tentative decision. The court heard argument from Mr. Owens regarding the negligent interference claim and alleged violations of Civil Code Sections 2924(g), (h), and (m). After hearing oral argument, the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is GRANTED with and without leave to amend. As a preliminary matter, the court notes that this matter was continued from April 8, 2025 to April 14, 2025 for Plaintiff to provide a court courtesy copy of their opposition by April 8 with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. Plaintiff failed to do so. Consequently, the filed opposition was not considered.
The demurrer to the First Cause of Action for Intentional Interference with Prospective Economic Advantage is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must allege facts that tend to show the probability of future economic benefit, rather than a mere speculative expectancy. (See Youst v. Longo (1987) 43 Cal.3d 64.) For example, it is not clear whether Drum was the only bidder.
The demurrer to the second cause of action for Negligent Interference with Prospective Economic Advantage is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must allege facts supporting a special relationship between Plaintiff and each individual defendant.
The demurrer to the third cause of action for violations as to Civil Code sections 2924g and 2024h is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must allege facts supporting a violation of each statute with reference to specific subdivisions. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend with respect to section 2924m. (See Civil Code Section 2924m(j).)

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order in accordance with CRC Rule 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-14-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Dustin Owens for Happy 42nd Partners, LLC, Plaintiff (via Zoom).
Eric M. Schiffer for Ryan Drum, Defendant (via Zoom).

Mr. Schiffer contested the court's tentative decision. Mr. Schiffer argued that the request for punitive damages should be stricken because there was no fraud cause of action alleged in the amended complaint. Mr. Owens responded that while the fraud cause of action had been removed, the factual allegations could still support punitive damages under Civil Code Section 3294 due to alleged fraudulent conduct not requiring reliance under that statute's definition. After hearing oral argument, the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is GRANTED in part with leave to amend.
Defendant's motion to strike paragraph 24 and paragraph 2 of the prayer for relief is denied. Plaintiff alleges facts supporting a claim for punitive damages by alleging Defendants knowingly submitted a fraudulent bid under penalty of perjury in order to obtain the subject property.
Defendant's motion to strike paragraph 7 of the prayer for relief requesting attorney's fees is granted with leave to amend. Plaintiff must allege entitlement to attorney fees either by contract, statute, or law.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order in accordance with CRC Rule 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
